purpose: Define a clear ownership split so service teams can move quickly without fragmenting core MCP contracts.
central_ownership: Risk tier and approval tier mapping.
Registry and pinned reference hash policy.
service_ownership: Runtime adapters or clients that invoke approved MCP contracts.
Service-local wiring such as auth header, timeout, retry, and trace propagation.
non_negotiable_rules: MCP IDs must use core-* naming in the central registry and service allowlists.
pinned_ref_hash must point to a deterministic runtime binding artifact.
Service repositories must not create independent MCP contract IDs that bypass the central registry.
Capability addition or removal is a central governance change and must update the registry, MCP manifest, and service allowlists.

conditions: Service-only MCP is temporary transition only.
The exception must be marked transitional.
A migration plan to core-* is required.
